Article Title: How to Convert Your HCL Commerce Into a Mobile App
SEO Meta Description: Embrace the mobile ecommerce trend and transform your HCL Commerce store into a mobile app! The MobiLoud app builder makes it easy and affordable to enhance your reach, increase conversions, and drive revenue growth through your very own HCL Commerce mobile app.
Content:
Embrace the mobile ecommerce trend and transform your HCL Commerce store into a mobile app to enhance your reach, increase conversions, and drive revenue growth.

Mobile commerce is on the rise, with a growing number of people using their smartphones for online shopping. If you're only focused on the desktop experience, you're neglecting a large portion of your customer base. Creating a fully custom native app can be expensive, but there's an alternative solution. Take advantage of an HCL Commerce mobile app builder to develop a fully-functional, top-quality mobile app for your business without breaking the bank.

Keep reading to learn how to turn your HCL Commerce store into a mobile app.

Why You Need a Mobile App

In 2022, over 72% of the ecommerce market share belonged to mobile - marking a significant surge above the 52% share in the five years prior.

This aligns with the general trend of mobile internet usage, which grows every year. Currently, mobile holds a market share of over 50% compared to desktop and tablet.

Further illustrating this shift, 15% of Americans today solely rely on smartphones as their means of accessing the internet. This represents the future of ecommerce, where customers are not just mobile-first, but mobile-only.

While optimizing your site for mobile is a good starting point, more is required to keep up with the ecommerce space. Mobile apps deliver a superior user experience, leading to improvements in several key metrics, including:

     
  • Enhanced conversion rates
    •  
  • Increased average order value
    •  
  • Higher product engagement per session

Additional data demonstrates that users of ecommerce mobile apps are twice as likely to revisit the store within 30 days compared to those using mobile browsers.

Mobile apps also provide the advantage of utilizing push notifications, which significantly increase everything from opt-in rates to open rates.

It's undeniable that mobile apps have become and excellent tool for nurturing loyal, repeat customers. And a mobile app tailored to HCL Commerce can yield significant benefits for your business.


Can You Create Mobile Apps for HCL Commerce?

If you're looking to create a mobile app for your HCL Commerce store, you won't be able to do it directly on the platform. HCL Commerce is specifically designed for webstores. However, there are ways to work around this limitation. Here are some options to consider:

  1. Find a third-party HCL Commerce mobile app builder that can help you build a mobile app.
  2. Explore tech-enabled assistance services that can transform your HCL Commerce store into a mobile application.
  3. Enlist the help of a team of skilled engineers who specialize in app development for Android and iOS. They can create mobile apps and connect them to your HCL Commerce shop.

Can I Turn My HCL Commerce Store into an App?

With the methods outlined earlier, you can seamlessly transform your HCL Commerce store into a mobile app.

Bear in mind, however, that the majority of store owners won't be able to afford the third option of hiring developers to build native apps. Developing a native app can range anywhere from $30,000 to $150,000, and that's just for one operating system! To launch apps for both Android and iOS, which is highly recommended, this cost would need to be doubled.

App development is a tricky process right from the start. It becomes even more challenging when your developers need to create an API and connect the backend of your apps to your HCL Commerce store.

It's likely that you don't have the budget for this - and that's not even considering the yearly costs of keeping your app running.

The Recurring Cost of Having an App

The yearly expenses that come with launching an app are often overlooked. Maintaining and keeping the app up-to-date, including tasks like operating system updates, HCL Commerce updates, bug fixes, implementing new features, and making improvements typically amounts to at least 15-20% of the initial cost each year.

A lot of times, those costs can be even higher. David Cost, the VP of Marketing at Rainbow Apparel, realized this reality during their app launch, stating:

"If we had an unlimited amount of time and money, we would probably opt for a custom native app. However, the maintenance alone would range from half a million to a million dollars per year."

Let's take a conservative approach and assume that building apps for both mobile platforms costs $150,000. Based on this estimate, the yearly expenses for updates and maintenance would be around $30,000.

For most store owners, this amount is too substantial to invest. Additionally, managing multiple platforms going forward, rather than just your website, can be a significant source of frustration. It triples the workload every time you need to make changes to your site.

The Best Way to Turn Your HCL Commerce Store into an App

Why Create an App For Your Healthcare Business?

With the rise of smartphones and the growing demand for convenience, developing a mobile app for your healthcare business is a smart move. A mobile app can significantly increase patient satisfaction, improve health outcomes, and foster long-term loyalty, making it a valuable investment for any healthcare business. 

Let’s dive into how a mobile app can help boost retention and engagement for your healthcare business:

1. Personalized Communication Keeps Patients Engaged

One of the coolest things about mobile apps is their ability to offer a personalized experience. Healthcare apps can send patients reminders about appointments, medication schedules, or even general health tips based on their specific needs. For example, a patient with diabetes might get reminders to check their blood sugar, while someone else might receive tips on managing stress or staying active.

This level of personalization makes patients feel cared for and supported, which naturally keeps them more engaged. Push notifications can also help bring patients back to the app by reminding them of important tasks, boosting retention and making sure they stay on track with their health.

2. Convenience Means Higher Retention

No one likes sitting on hold or waiting in line. Mobile apps make accessing healthcare services super convenient. With just a few taps, patients can book appointments, view their medical records, or even chat with their healthcare provider. Some apps also offer telemedicine options, so patients can get the care they need without leaving their homes.

When healthcare is this easy to access, patients are more likely to stick with a provider. They appreciate the convenience, and that keeps them coming back for more.

3. Better Outcomes with Data-Driven Insights

Many healthcare apps can connect with wearable devices or track data like heart rate, steps, or sleep patterns. This data can be shared with healthcare providers, helping them offer more personalized care. For patients, knowing that their doctor is keeping an eye on their progress can be incredibly motivating.

When patients see that their data is being used to offer better care, they’re more likely to stay engaged. Plus, apps that provide real-time feedback based on this data create a sense of partnership between the patient and provider. That’s a big win for retention.

4. Self-Management Tools Empower Patients

Mobile apps are packed with features that help patients take control of their own health. From symptom trackers to medication reminders, these tools give patients more control over their treatment. When people feel empowered and confident about managing their health, they’re much more likely to stay engaged with both the app and their healthcare provider.

The convenience of having everything in one place, such as health tips, appointment schedules, or access to medical records, means patients are less likely to look elsewhere for care. This sense of empowerment and ease drives loyalty.

5. Telehealth = More Engagement

Telehealth has been a game-changer in the healthcare industry. Many healthcare apps now include telehealth features, allowing patients to consult with their doctor virtually. This flexibility is huge. Patients love the option to get care without leaving the house, especially if they live far away or have mobility issues.

By offering telehealth services, healthcare providers can expand their reach and keep patients engaged without requiring an in-person visit. When patients appreciate the convenience and quick access to care, they’re far more likely to stick with that provider.

6. Loyalty Programs and Rewards Build Long-Term Relationships

Everyone loves rewards, and healthcare apps can leverage this to boost engagement. By incorporating loyalty programs, healthcare businesses can encourage patients to stick with their treatment plans. Offering discounts on services or providing rewards for attending regular check-ups can motivate patients to stay engaged with the app and their provider.

These small incentives create a sense of accomplishment and reinforce the idea that patients are actively taking care of their health. In the long run, this builds a stronger relationship between the patient and the healthcare provider, driving retention.

Wrapping It Up

Healthcare mobile apps are a game-changer when it comes to keeping patients engaged and loyal.

From personalized communication and easy access to services, to self-management tools and telehealth options, these apps offer a wide range of benefits that make healthcare more convenient and enjoyable for patients.

App Description: See why cookware, furniture, and décor brands are turning to mobile apps. Learn the fastest way to launch iOS and Android apps without rebuilding your site.
Article Title: How to Build a Mobile App for Home & Kitchen Brands
SEO Meta Description: Your best customers are already mobile-first, and expect an app experience that makes browsing, bundling, and reordering effortless. This guide shows Home & Kitchen brands the fastest, lowest-risk path to a high-performing app using MobiLoud’s fully managed website-to-app service, with proof points, process, and an action plan.
Content:
Selling cookware, furniture, housewares, or home décor online isn’t the same as selling a T-shirt. Customers browse deeper, compare sets, and return multiple times before making a purchase. Many also reorder essentials, like kitchen tools, appliances, or décor pieces, again and again.

Yet on mobile web, these experiences are clunky, slow, and easy to abandon. Whether it’s cookware reorders, browsing furniture collections, or finding the perfect décor piece, customers want speed, convenience, and a direct connection to the brands they trust.

A mobile app delivers exactly that. It puts your store on the home screen, makes reordering seamless, and opens a powerful new channel with push notifications. 

With MobiLoud, you can launch fully branded iOS and Android apps in weeks – without rebuilding your site or taking on the cost and complexity of custom development.

Why Cookware, Furniture, and Home Décor Brands Need Mobile Apps

Home & Kitchen ecommerce is booming, but competition is intense. Mobile now accounts for the majority of traffic. Yet mobile websites often struggle with conversion, speed, and retention. 

For cookware, furniture, housewares, and décor brands, that means missed opportunities with high-intent buyers.

Mobile apps solve this gap. Industry benchmarks show that ecommerce apps:

  • Convert 3x higher than mobile websites
  • Generate 10-50% higher average order values
  • Deliver up to 4x more revenue per user than mobile web

Push notifications amplify this further. Our data finds that they drive 3-5x higher click-through rates than email, with average conversion rates of ~4%.

Certain flows, such as abandoned cart notifications, perform exceptionally well, with conversion rates up to 22% in some cases.

Push notifications are a powerful channel to drive repeat purchases, announce back-in-stock furniture, or nudge customers to reorder cookware and housewares.

For verticals like Home & Kitchen, where customers browse deeply, compare, and often buy in sets, apps are especially powerful. 

They make product discovery smoother, checkout faster with native payments, and reordering just a tap away. 

That translates into higher revenue per customer, stronger loyalty, and a durable edge over brands that rely solely on mobile web.

Home & Kitchen Mobile App Development: 3 Options Compared (Cookware, Décor, Housewares, Furniture)

When Home & Kitchen brands decide to launch an app, they usually consider three paths: hiring a native agency, using a template builder, or converting their website into apps with MobiLoud. Each comes with trade-offs in cost, speed, and long-term ROI.

1. Native App Development (Agencies or In-House)

The traditional path to building an app is hiring an agency or in-house developers to design and code fully native iOS and Android applications from scratch.

At first, this can sound appealing. You get complete creative freedom and the ability to craft completely bespoke experiences.

However, the investment and long-term complexity of building a custom app is too much for most brands.

Pros:

  • Maximum flexibility and control over the look, feel, and functionality of the app.
  • Ability to build unique, powerful features (for example, AR for furniture placement).
  • Full ownership of the codebase.

Cons:

  • Cost: Native apps are expensive. Agencies often quote six figures per platform just to launch, with ongoing development and maintenance on top. For most Home & Kitchen brands, the ROI simply isn’t there.
  • Time: Custom development projects can take 6–12 months or longer. That’s a year of opportunity cost where competitors may already be reaping the benefits of app revenue.
  • Complexity: Every update to your ecommerce store needs to be duplicated in the app. If you tweak your cookware PDP design, add a new furniture collection layout, or update checkout flows, developers need to manually replicate those changes in the native codebase.
  • Maintenance burden: Keeping up with iOS and Android updates, app store requirements, and bug fixes requires a dedicated product and engineering team.

For most Home & Kitchen brands, this level of investment and ongoing complexity doesn’t make sense.

Unless you’re a global enterprise with unlimited budget and a product vision that truly demands unique native functionality, the cost and time outweigh the benefits.

2. Template App Builders

Another common option is using a template-based app builder. These platforms are marketed to Shopify or WooCommerce merchants as a quick way to turn your store into an app. 

You pick from a set of pre-made templates, drag and drop modules, and connect your catalog to auto-populate the app.

At first glance, this can feel like a low-cost shortcut. You get into the App Store and Google Play quickly, without paying agency rates. 

For very simple stores, that can be enough. But Home & Kitchen brands rarely have “simple” ecommerce needs. 

Cookware companies rely on bundles, furniture brands have large catalogs with variants and customization, and décor or housewares stores depend on content and merchandising to inspire purchases. Template builders usually struggle to handle this complexity.

Pros:

  • Lower upfront costs compared to native development.
  • Faster initial launch timeline (often measured in weeks).
  • Easy setup with drag-and-drop tools, no coding required.

Cons:

  • Rigid templates: These platforms force you into a limited set of layouts. Your carefully optimized PDPs, furniture lookbooks, or cookware bundle flows won’t transfer over cleanly.
  • Feature gaps: Third-party integrations like reviews, loyalty, or financing tools often don’t carry through. For Home & Kitchen brands that rely on trust signals and upsell mechanics, that’s a major drawback.
  • Separate content management: Many builders require you to manage content twice; once in your site’s CMS and again in the app dashboard. That duplication creates extra work and inconsistencies over time.
  • Design trade-offs: Apps built this way often feel generic. For brands selling high-value furniture or premium kitchenware, a cookie-cutter experience undermines brand positioning.
  • Scalability limits: As your business grows, you may quickly hit the ceiling of what the platform can do. Migrating off later usually means starting from scratch.

For cookware, furniture, and home décor brands that depend on merchandising, storytelling, and a seamless customer journey, a DIY builder will not be enough.

Template builders are best suited for small stores with basic catalogs, not established Home & Kitchen retailers looking to build loyalty and grow repeat revenue.

3. MobiLoud: Website-to-App Service

MobiLoud takes a different approach from both custom development and template app builders. 

Instead of rebuilding your ecommerce store in native code or forcing it into a rigid template, MobiLoud transforms your existing website into fully native iOS and Android apps.

The site you already invested in becomes the foundation of your app – with nothing lost in the process.

This matters a lot for Home & Kitchen brands. Your website isn’t just a catalog, it’s a finely tuned sales engine. 

It likely includes custom PDP layouts for cookware sets, lookbooks and inspiration galleries for furniture and décor, upsell flows for housewares, and integrations with reviews, loyalty, and financing tools. 

With MobiLoud, all of that comes through intact. No compromises, no duplication, no rebuilding.

How it works:

MobiLoud converts your existing mobile website into a native app, adding the mobile app-specific features that matter most, like push notifications, a native tab bar, splash screens, and app-only settings. 

We handle everything: design configuration, testing, QA, app store submission, and ongoing updates. You continue running your website as usual, and the app automatically stays in sync.

Pros:

  • No rebuild required: Your entire site powers the app, so you keep all your merchandising, PDPs, checkout flows, and custom features.
  • Fast launch: Apps are typically live in ~4 weeks, compared to months for agencies.
  • Done-for-you service: We handle design, configuration, publishing, and ongoing maintenance. Your team doesn’t need technical knowledge or spare bandwidth.
  • Full branding and polish: Apps are customized to feel fully native and on-brand, not like a cookie-cutter template.
  • Scalable: Works with any ecommerce platform or tech stack, so you’re not locked into Shopify-only or limited ecosystems.
  • Ongoing support: Our team acts as an extension of yours, helping with push strategy, analytics, and growth after launch.

Cons:

  • Because the app mirrors your site, it isn’t meant for brands looking to build a completely separate native experience.

For cookware, furniture, and home décor brands, MobiLoud strikes the ideal balance. You get into the App Store and Google Play quickly, you unlock the powerful engagement of a mobile app, and you do it without the cost, complexity, or compromises of the other two approaches. 

The result is an app that looks and feels fully native, drives revenue from day one, and requires almost no ongoing lift from your team.

Want to see what’s possible? Get a free preview of your app now.

Why MobiLoud is the Best Choice for Home & Kitchen, Cookware, and Furniture App Development

Article Title: 4 Ways to Build a Home & Garden Mobile eCommerce App
SEO Meta Description: Want to build a home & garden mobile app for iOS and Android? Mobile eCommerce apps can take your brand to the next level - find out how to build them with MobiLoud.
Content:
To successfully build home and garden ecommerce apps, there are several viable options. The right one for you depends on exactly what you want, how much time you have, the size of your budget, and several other factors.

In this article we're going to guide you to make the right choice, explaining the key considerations and ultimately arguing that MobiLoud is your best option. Let's get started with some encouraging data.

Home and Garden as an eCommerce category is growing fast. The home e-commerce sector is expected to grow by USD 25.88 billion from 2020 to 2025, at a CAGR of 9.64% during the forecast period (source).

More generally, eCommerce as a whole is booming, with sales predicted to grow 10.4% this year. By 2026, eCommerce is expected to make up 24% of all retail purchases.

Finally the explosion in mobile app usage, with its market size projected to reach $407 Billion by 2026, underlines the immense potential of home and garden ecommerce apps  for scaling your business.

Several Home and Garden brands are already seeing real success with eCommerce apps. Let’s look at an impressive home and garden ecommerce apps example to see what's possible.

Wayfair Mobile Apps

Great vertical brands like Wayfair have been building solid mobile apps for years.

Wayfair's app offers a vast selection of home goods, furniture, and decor items, catering to diverse styles and budgets. The app is known for its augmented reality (AR) feature, which allows users to visualize how items would look in their homes before purchasing. Additional features include access to daily sales, thousands of product reviews, a 'Room Ideas' section for inspiration, and an easy-to-use interface.

Wayfair have had success with iOS and Android apps, and you can too. Let’s look at how you can also build even better apps in the next section.

How to build home and garden ecommerce apps

Building home and garden ecommerce apps will allow you to:

  • Improve mobile UX
  • Boost up sales
  • Increase customer LTV

Mobile ecommerce apps are huge for many brands. The ability to send push notifications alone is a game changer.

Sounds good, right? Right. The real issue is building them. It can be a challenge - depending on the route you choose to take.

Speaking broadly, there are 4 options:

  • Native development
  • Hybrid development
  • App builders
  • MobiLoud

Let’s look at them each in turn.

Native App Development

Native app development involves creating an application specifically for a particular platform, like iOS or Android, using languages and development tools supported by those platforms. For iOS, it's Swift or Objective-C with Xcode, and for Android, it's Java or Kotlin with Android Studio.

Pros:

  • High Performance: Native apps run smoothly as they are built specifically for a single platform.
  • Better Integration: They offer seamless integration with the hardware (like camera, GPS, etc.) and software features of the device.
  • UX: Native apps can provide a superior user experience, adhering to the specific UI standards of each platform.

Cons:

  • High Costs: Building separate apps for each platform doubles development and maintenance costs.
  • Lead time: Each app must be created separately, consuming more resources and time.
  • Overall native development is a possibility. Overall though, it's unclear whether its worth it.

Good native apps will cost six figures, and take six months to build, at an absolute minimum.

This alone prices out most small and medium sized businesses, and is a long time to wait before going to market.

There's no need to reinvent the wheel to build home and garden ecommerce apps . What you need is a smooth UX, the right features, and a fast and efficient build. This is better achieved through other, less-risky methods.

Hybrid App Development

Hybrid apps blend elements of both native and web apps. They're capable of running on different platforms using the same code. In the context of home and garden ecommerce apps , they can theoretically be built with frameworks like React Native & Flutter, tools that allow you to build single apps compatible with iOS and Android.

Pros

  • Faster Development: Since the same codebase can be used for multiple platforms, development is quicker than native development.
  • Cost-Effective: The need for separate teams for different platforms is eliminated, reducing costs.

Cons

  • Lower Performance: Hybrid apps can't match the speed of native apps because they need to work across multiple platforms.
  • Expense: though more affordable than native development, building good hybrid home and garden ecommerce apps  will still cost tens of thousands of dollars, upfront
  • Time: though faster than native development, you'll still have a complex project to manage for many months

Hybrid apps are a possibility, but suffer from the same flaw as native development - time, expense, and risk. You will need to be at least somewhat technical to even understand if your idea is feasible.

The technicalities of both hybrid and native development are beyond the scope of this article, we covered them briefly just to make you aware of your broad options.

Next we'll get into a few options for building home and garden ecommerce apps with much lower time and capital investment.

App Builders

App builders are software that allow users to create mobile applications using drag-and-drop interfaces.

In the eCommerce space there are typically two main types.

The first are based on blocks, allowing you to drag-and-drop various pre-built components to define the UI of your apps. Different app builders will have different blocks for specific functions and integrations with some third parties. There will also be some customisation options like colour schemes and logos which will help it to look like something vaguely resembling your main web store.

The second main type of app builder is based on templates or themes. You'll be able to choose from one of their pre-built themes, which come with different styles and functionalities, then add different pre-built widgets and styling to achieve some degree of customization.

In reality, many app builders combine elements of both. What they have in common are pre-built UIs with limited customization options, and a reliance on the APIs of whatever you use as a CMS.

App builders are a popular choice for non-technical individuals or small businesses looking to develop simple apps and there are several in the eCommerce space.

Pros

  • No Coding Required: App builders are user-friendly, requiring no coding skills
  • Fast and Affordable: Users can quickly create and publish an app on multiple platforms

Cons

  • Limited Customization: App builders have a limited set of templates and features, restricting unique customization
  • Performance Issues: Apps made with builders may not perform as well as native or hybrid apps

App builders can be a decent choice, if you aren't too concerned with performance or customisation.

They come with serious limitations though. What makes them fast and cheap - reliance on APIs, templates, and widgets - is also what makes them inflexible and generic.

They force your brand into one of these templates then use APIs to populate them, you'll never have much freedom. APIs only expose certain data, templates can only approximate your brand. If you have a feature on your site and they don't have the perfect pre-built integration available, you can't use it. Even if they do, these various integrations have a performance record that is far from perfect.

APIs also come with platform risk. All it needs is one major update to turn your workflows and app functionality upside down.

“The hybrid solutions on top of Shopify are just too limited. The Shopify API only enables certain things and for all the major providers of hybrid apps for Shopify, they had major limitations that the Shopify API would not let them get around. When our first wrapper app stopped working, we were forced into working with another app provider. That was a year of work, and then we discovered all the different things they couldn’t do because of the Shopify API.”

  - David Cost, VP of Ecommerce and Marketing at Rainbow (read their story)

So app builders are way more affordable, and far simpler to use. You can get an app in just hours, and probably afford their pricing if you have any revenue whatsoever. But, if you have any degree of complexity or performance on your web store, the apps will only be a pale imitation.

We're now going to talk about our platform, MobiLoud, and show you how it gives you the best of both worlds - the efficiency and affordability of app builders with the flexibility and UX of native apps.

MobiLoud is the best way to build home and garden ecommerce apps, let's see how.

MobiLoud

You can spend $150k+ and six months plus building native home and garden ecommerce apps  and get something quality, but still limited.

Alternatively, you can tinker with cheap app builders based on APIs and templates, but they’ll limit you even more and could easily be a complete waste of time.

You don’t just want an app, just to tick a box. You want something great. You want something at least as good, if not better, than your main web store. Something that will have a measurable impact on driving up conversions, sales, and ultimately profits.

MobiLoud is a unique answer to the challenge, combining the best of all 3 categories above.

MobiLoud converts your existing Home and Garden site to iOS and Android apps through a native "wrapper", and everything from your site will work in the apps.

We really mean everything. No matter which themes, plugins, or 3rd party tools you use for your site - they will work flawlessly in the apps, which will sync with your site and update automatically.

With MobiLoud you don’t need to worry about whether you can replicate the features, design, and functionality of your site. You can. Your Home and Garden site already works. It's already optimized. You’ve already spent significant time and money tweaking everything, so you already have what you need.

With MobiLoud you don't need to commit yourself to any long and expensive projects. You just configure some settings, and we handle all technical aspects of the app build. Within just a few weeks you'll be ready to launch on iOS and Android (which we handle too), and going forward we handle all ongoing updates and maintenance.

“It was an easy experience. I've always got a million projects on my plate and with this I was able to spin up an app in two months for a couple $1000. It made me look really good. I like everything about the experience.”

 - Brent Stimmel, VP of IT at JF Petroleum (read their story)

There’s no chance you get apps you aren’t happy with, because we’ll refund you in the unlikely event that happens. We don't do annual contracts either, so you can cancel at any time.

Going forward you can manage the apps as easily as you do your site, without needing any technical knowledge. The tricky parts are all handled by us - so you don't need to add extra employees or stress your team out.

We've already built apps for thousands of businesses, including dozens of eCommerce sites. Check out these stories:

  • Rainbow Shops
  • SYN.DI.CATE
  • Bestseller
  • John Varvatos

These brands got top quality apps with MobiLoud, saving themselves tens of thousands of dollars and months of effort. You can too. Let’s build your home and garden ecommerce apps, book a call today and we’ll explain everything.

Article Title: How to Build a Mobile App for Your Hygraph Website
SEO Meta Description: Want to build an app for your Hygraph website? MobiLoud is the best way to do it. Learn why, and how to go from website to app in just a couple of weeks, in this article.
Content:
Hygraph sits at the center of your content architecture. It's where your editorial team manages product stories, landing pages, campaign content, and rich media, all delivered through a single GraphQL API that feeds your frontend.

But Hygraph is your content layer, not your entire stack. You've paired it with a commerce engine for checkout and orders, a search provider like Algolia, a payment processor, and a frontend framework (probably Next.js) that stitches it all together. 

If you're using Content Federation, you might be pulling product data, pricing, and inventory from multiple external APIs into one unified endpoint.

That's a lot of moving parts. And now you want a native mobile app.

The good news: you don't need to rebuild any of it. Your website already has everything, the content, the commerce logic, the integrations, the UX. You just need a way to deliver that same experience as a native app in the App Store and Google Play.

Can You Build a Mobile App with Hygraph?

Not with Hygraph alone. 

Hygraph is a headless CMS, it manages and delivers content, but it doesn't handle checkout, payments, inventory, or any of the transactional side of ecommerce. And it doesn't include a mobile app builder.

What about Hygraph's "App Framework"?

Hygraph does have an App Framework, but it's for building CMS extensions, things like custom fields, sidebar widgets, and editorial tools. It has nothing to do with consumer-facing mobile apps.

What about your commerce engine's app builders?

This depends on which commerce platform you've paired with Hygraph. If you're using Shopify as your commerce backend, there are third-party app builders in the Shopify ecosystem. 

But here's the catch: those builders work with Shopify's Liquid templates and Storefront API. They don't know anything about your Hygraph content, your Content Federation setup, or your custom Next.js frontend. You'd end up with an app that shows products but misses all the editorial content and custom experiences that make your site yours.

If you're paired with Commercetools, BigCommerce, or Spryker, there's no real app builder ecosystem at all.

PWA?

Your Next.js frontend can be configured as a PWA. 

But you already know the limitations: the add-to-home-screen process is unintuitive, very few customers actually install it, there's no App Store presence, and push notification support on iOS is limited.

Your Options for Launching a Mobile App

For brands running on Hygraph, there are two real paths to launching a (real) mobile app.

Custom Native or Cross-Platform Development

You can build a native iOS and Android app (or use React Native or Flutter) that queries your Hygraph GraphQL API and your commerce API directly.

In 2023, mobile eCommerce sales reached $2.2 trillion and made up about 60% of all eCommerce sales around the world. This global trend is expected to even grow bigger in 2027. Internal communication apps can be a great help to businesses, especially in this new era of remote-first, distributed organizations.

How do you build one that covers all your needs though?

We’re going to show you how you can build a custom internal communication app in record time with our platform MobiLoud.

We’re going to make two assumptions:

  1. You already have a web based communication tool
  2. If not, you’re willing to build one

Why these assumptions? To find out, let’s take a look at how MobiLoud works.

What is MobiLoud

MobiLoud is a web to app platform for converting any website or web app into iOS and Android apps.

MobiLoud is used by hundreds of startups and international brands – and also works perfectly for internal management and communication tools.

It doesn’t matter what CMS or platform you used to build your web tool, MobiLoud will turn it into mobile apps while retaining the exact features and functionality of the originals.

The apps will also sync completely with your existing platform, updating automatically, so there’s no extra work for you in maintaining it.

How does MobiLoud work?

MobiLoud creates what is known as “hybrid apps”. Hybrid apps mix native and web elements to build apps faster and more efficiently.

MobiLoud uses what are known as “webviews” to display the content from your web app in a native “wrapper”.

Think of it like a dedicated browser that can be installed on the device, published on the App Store and Google Play, and has all the native elements added to ensure a great user experience.

You can read more about the principle in this article by the founder of Basecamp – “native navigation – web content”.

Lot’s of major tech companies use this approach, according to UX Matters:

“We know that Facebook, Evernote, Instagram, LinkedIn, Uber, Slack, Twitter, Gmail, the Amazon Appstore, and many others are or have been WebView apps”

It works equally well for internal communication platforms. We take what you already have, convert it to mobile apps (in two weeks) – then the apps are ready for your team members to use.

You’ll save tens of thousands of dollars compared to custom development, and months of effort and hassle.

But what if you haven’t already built an internal communication platform on the web? Read on and we’ll give you our advice.

What if I don’t have a web platform already?

If you have not already built an internal communication platform for the web – it might be a good time to build one!

It’s better to go cross-platform rather than trying to just build a mobile app version so that your team can access it easily on both desktop and mobile.

Building internal communication tools used to be a large development project, but thanks to low-code and no-code tools it’s easier than ever.

Many internal communication apps have functionality like:

  • Hosting a knowledge base, wiki, SOPs, training materials etc
  • Team networking/messenger/chat functionality

  • Calendars
  • Key announcements and company news

You can build all of this in WordPress, or use a no-code app builder like Bubble for the job. When you’ve built it you could use ToDesktop to convert it into a convenient desktop app, then use MobiLoud to build iOS and Android apps that replicate the exact functionality!

No matter what features, functionality, or existing content your internal communication platform has – MobiLoud will replicate it exactly right out of the box.

Simple management

You’ll only need to maintain one codebase, and won’t need to rely on expensive and specialised mobile developers.

The apps will be low maintenance, and will update in real time with your web platform. There’s no work to add for you or your team!

Launch fast, save thousands

Contracting an app agency to build custom apps would cost tens of thousands and likely take months.

With MobiLoud you can launch in just a few weeks, and keep costs at a reasonable level – see our pricing here.  

Full service

MobiLoud isn’t just a software tool, it’s a full service.

We’re with you every step of the way to get the apps built and launched, and we help you to get them published on the App Store and Google Play.

It doesn’t end there though – as we also handle all future updates and maintenance on the apps and make sure they keep running smoothly.

If you aren’t happy with the apps for any reason, we’ll refund you.

Full Featured

Your MobiLoud apps will have all the features you need. For example, you’ll have unlimited push notifications that you can use to notify company employees of important announcements and updates.

To learn more about MobiLoud’s features, check out this page and the FAQ section.

Start building your internal communication apps today!

  • If you don’t already have one, you can build it for the web then use MobiLoud for apps

We’ll go through the whole process, answer any questions, and you’ll get all the information you need to decide whether MobiLoud is right for you.

App Description: Intershop is an enterprise ecommerce platform designed for complex, high-scale B2B and hybrid commerce operations.
Article Title: How to Launch a Native Mobile App for Your Intershop Commerce Site
SEO Meta Description: Enhance conversions and search visibility by converting your Intershop store into a mobile app. Read on to learn how MobiLoud makes it easy.
Content:
Your Intershop implementation is doing serious work. Account-specific pricing, complex catalog structures, approval workflows, SAP integration, SPARQUE.AI-powered search, customer portals with order history and reordering. 

This isn't a simple storefront. It's the digital backbone of how your business buyers interact with you.

And increasingly, those buyers want to do it from their phones. Field sales reps need catalog access on site visits. Procurement teams want to reorder from wherever they are. Account managers need to check order status and pricing without opening a laptop.

Intershop's answer to mobile is their Progressive Web App. It's a solid piece of technology. 

But if you've been evaluating whether a PWA is truly enough, or if you already have a PWA live and your adoption numbers aren't where they should be, you're not alone.

There's a path to a fully native app in the App Store and Google Play that doesn't require rebuilding your Intershop implementation. And it takes weeks, not months.

Can You Build a Mobile App with Intershop?

Not a native one. Intershop's mobile strategy is built entirely around Progressive Web Apps. Here's what that looks like:

Intershop's Angular PWA

Intershop provides an open-source Angular-based PWA as the standard storefront frontend. It's well-built, actively maintained, and MIT-licensed. It supports responsive design, home screen installation, and some offline capabilities.

If you're already running Intershop, there's a good chance your storefront is already a PWA. On paper, that checks the "mobile" box.

In practice, PWA adoption among actual users tends to be low. 

The add-to-home-screen process is not intuitive, and very few customers go through with it. There's no App Store or Google Play presence, so buyers can't search for your brand and download an app the way they would with any other business tool. Push notification support is unreliable on iOS (it only works if the PWA is already installed to the home screen, which, as noted, most users don't do). 

And the experience, while functional, doesn't feel native in the way business users increasingly expect.

For B2B, this matters more than you might think. Your buyers use native apps for everything else in their workflow: Slack, Teams, their ERP mobile client, their expense tool. A PWA that behaves slightly differently from everything else on their phone creates friction, even if it's subtle.

APIs for custom development

Intershop is API-first with a microservices architecture. You could build a fully custom native app using these APIs. But that's a major development project, not a feature you can turn on. 

Every piece of your B2B storefront (account-specific catalogs, pricing rules, approval workflows, order templates, SPARQUE.AI search) would need to be rebuilt in a native frontend.

No app builder ecosystem

There are no third-party mobile app builders for Intershop. The ecosystem is focused on web commerce, integration (especially SAP), and AI-powered merchandising. If you want a native app, your options are custom development or a website-to-app approach.

Your Options for Launching a Mobile App

For Intershop customers, there are three realistic paths to build your own mobile app.

Stay with your PWA

You already have one (or could deploy one). Intershop's Angular PWA is a legitimate mobile experience, and for some businesses it's sufficient. 

If your buyers are primarily desktop users who occasionally check orders on mobile, a PWA may cover the basics.

But if mobile adoption is a strategic priority, if you want your brand in the App Store, if you need reliable push notifications across iOS and Android, or if your field sales team needs a dedicated mobile tool, a PWA has a real ceiling.

Custom native development

You could build native iOS and Android apps (or use React Native or Flutter) connected to Intershop's APIs.

For an enterprise B2B commerce app, this typically runs $150,000 to $400,000+ for the initial build. 

B2B complexity makes it expensive: account-specific pricing, custom catalogs per buyer group, approval workflows, quote management, order templates, and deep ERP integration all need to be rebuilt in the mobile frontend. 

On top of that, annual maintenance runs $50,000 to $100,000+ to keep pace with OS updates, app store requirements, and parity with your web storefront.

Then there's the operational cost: two separate frontends to maintain. Every change to your Intershop storefront (new product data, updated pricing rules, revised catalog structure, new promotions) has to be replicated in the app. 

Most teams eventually deprioritize the app. It falls behind the website and slowly falls into disrepair, right when your buyers have started depending on it.

Custom development makes sense if your app needs to do things your web storefront fundamentally can't. For most Intershop customers, the goal is simpler: get the B2B buying experience you've already built into a native app.

Website-to-app with MobiLoud

There's a more practical path: take your existing Intershop storefront and extend it into a native mobile app, with MobiLoud.

MobiLoud takes the web storefront you've already built and delivers it as a native iOS and Android app. 

Your B2B commerce experience is the app. Account-specific pricing, catalogs, approval workflows, order templates, SPARQUE.AI search, everything carries over automatically.

On top of that, MobiLoud adds what your PWA can't reliably deliver: native push notifications, App Store and Google Play presence, native navigation, deep linking, and the feel of a real app on your buyers' devices.

The cost is a fraction of custom development. The launch timeline is weeks, not months. And because the app renders your live storefront, every update is immediately reflected in the app. No duplicate work.

Why MobiLoud is the Best Way for Intershop Customers to Launch an App

MobiLoud converts your existing Intershop storefront into native iOS and Android apps.

It gives you everything your PWA can't deliver, without the cost and complexity of a custom build.

Here's why this approach fits Intershop:

Your entire B2B commerce experience carries over

This is where it matters most. Your Intershop implementation isn't a simple product catalog. It has account-specific pricing, customer-group catalogs, approval workflows, order templates for repeat purchasing, quote management, and complex multi-step checkout. Rebuilding any of that in a custom native app takes months.

With MobiLoud, all of it carries over because the app delivers your existing storefront. Whatever your buyers can do on the web, they can do in the app.

SPARQUE.AI and your integrations work automatically

Your SPARQUE.AI-powered search and product recommendations, your SAP integration through the Integration Hub, your payment processing, your ERP-connected inventory and pricing: it all works in the app exactly as it works on the web. No API reconnection. No integration layer to rebuild.

If it works on your website, it works in your app.

Real App Store presence for your B2B brand

This is the gap your PWA can't fill. A native app in the App Store and Google Play means your buyers can search for your brand, download your app, and have it sitting alongside every other business tool on their phone.

For field sales teams, this is the difference between "open your browser and navigate to our portal" and "tap the app." For procurement teams reordering regularly, it's the difference between bookmarking a URL and having a dedicated app with a home screen icon and badge notifications.

B2B buyers are also B2C consumers. They expect the same convenience from their business tools that they get from everything else on their phone.

Native push notifications that actually work

PWA push notifications are unreliable on iOS and require the PWA to be installed to the home screen first. That's a narrow funnel.

Native push notifications work consistently across iOS and Android, regardless of whether the user has recently opened the app. For B2B, this means reliable delivery of order status updates, quote approvals, restock alerts, promotional pricing windows, and account notifications.

Multi-country, multi-language, multi-catalog

If your Intershop implementation serves multiple countries, languages, or buyer groups with different catalogs and pricing, all of that carries over to the app automatically.

MobiLoud specializes in helping global brands with complex setups. Whether you need a single app for all markets or separate apps for different regions, we can help you make it happen.

No new codebase to maintain

When your team updates the storefront, the app reflects it automatically. Updated pricing? It's in the app. New product line in the catalog? It's in the app. Revised approval workflow? It's in the app.

You're not coordinating releases across web, iOS, and Android. You make changes in one place and they're live everywhere.

Launch in weeks, not months

A custom Intershop mobile app takes 6-12+ months to build. MobiLoud apps typically launch in about 4 weeks. And you already know what you're going to get, because the app is a direct reflection of what already works on your website.

Fully managed service

MobiLoud handles everything: the build, app store submission (Apple and Google), ongoing maintenance, and support. Your team stays focused on your Intershop platform and your B2B operations. We handle everything to do with your app.

How It Works

The process is straightforward, and it doesn't require your development team to take on a new project.

  1. Get a free consultation. We'll put together an interactive preview so you can see exactly what your Intershop storefront looks like as a native app before committing to anything.
  2. App build. Once you give the go-ahead, we put your apps together. Native layer with push notifications, native navigation, app icon, splash screen, deep linking, and any extras needed to meet your requirements.
  3. Testing and QA. We run comprehensive tests across iOS and Android devices. You test on your own devices and provide feedback.
  4. App store submission. MobiLoud handles Apple App Store and Google Play submission, including all compliance requirements and metadata.
  5. Launch and ongoing support. Your app goes live. MobiLoud handles OS updates, app store policy changes, maintenance, and support on an ongoing basis. You keep working on your Intershop platform.

Your Intershop infrastructure isn't affected. Your commerce platform, your integrations, your SAP connection, your SPARQUE.AI setup: everything keeps running exactly as it does today. You just gain a native mobile channel for your buyers.

Next Steps

If you're running on Intershop and you want to go beyond your PWA with a fully native app, MobiLoud is the most practical way to make it happen.

The cost could be less than 1% of what it takes to build a custom B2B mobile app. And you don't need to pull your development team away from the projects they're already working on.

Your storefront already works. Your B2B workflows, your integrations, your catalogs, your pricing logic: it's all live and functional.

Why spend $200K+ and six months rebuilding all of that in a separate codebase when you can extend it into a native app directly?

See how it works, what your site could look like as an app, and get an honest assessment of whether this is the right move for your business, with a free consultation.

Article Title: How to Convert JavaScript to Mobile Apps for Android and iOS
SEO Meta Description: Learn why and how to convert a JavaScript web app into mobile apps, and why MobiLoud is the best way to do it.
Content:
Want to create a mobile app with JavaScript?

JavaScript is the most dominant programming language in the world today. It ranks as the number one programming language in Stack Overflow’s annual developer survey, and you can find JavaScript in the codebase of almost every website with even a small amount of interactivity.

But while you can build incredible, complex web apps with JavaScript, what about mobile apps?

You need an extra tool or programming language to create a JavaScript mobile app. JS is missing the native code needed for an app to run on mobile operating systems such as Android and iOS.

That means you need an additional tool or framework to bridge the gap. There are several options available to you:

  • Write a JavaScript web app, and use webviews/app wrappers to display your web content in a native app container.
  • Build a hybrid app using JavaScript frameworks like Ionic, Apache Cordova or React Native.
  • Convert a JavaScript web app into a mobile app using MobiLoud.

If you already have a web app written in JavaScript, and want to convert it into a mobile app, MobiLoud is the best way to do it.

It requires minimal rebuilding, no new programming languages to learn, and lets you easily carry everything over from the web app to mobile.

How to Build Mobile Apps with JavaScript

MobiLoud is the best way to convert your JavaScript web app into a mobile app. 

You can do it without any rebuilding, and while maintaining a single codebase for all of your apps.

Here’s how you can utilize familiar JavaScript, HTML and CSS code and still ship a feature-rich, native mobile app:

  1. Book a free demo to learn more about the process, and discuss the vision you have for the app and any specific wants or needs.‍
  2. Sign up for a plan (no long-term commitments or contracts required).
  3. Make your final configurations and hand off to our team to build your mobile apps.
  4. Test the apps and send us any last pieces of feedback or change requests.
  5. Wait for us to let you know that your apps are live and published in the app stores.
  6. Start promoting your new Android and iOS apps, powered by the same JavaScript codebase as your website.

You’ll save tens (or hundreds) of thousands and months of development time compared to JavaScript mobile frameworks, avoid the complexity and overhead of juggling multiple codebases, while still shipping a fast and full-featured mobile app.

If this sounds like the ideal outcome, get in touch with us and schedule a free consultation now.

To get the full picture, let's now run through some alternative methods to convert a web app built with JavaScript, HTML and CSS into a mobile app.

Create JavaScript Mobile Apps Using Webviews

Implementation-wise, the simplest way of making a JavaScript app work on mobile is by wrapping it in a webview.

Webviews are browser-like containers which render your HTML and JavaScript website as it is.

The mobile app will act as a container, with your your JavaScript web app running inside.

Although it's one of the easier options, creating a webview app is not a completely straightforward process.

Converting JavaScript to Android App with Webviews

To use webviews to build a JavaScript Android app, you must build a native app in Android Studio.

This requires Android development skills along with an in-depth knowledge of how webview apps work.

You’ll also need extensive experience in webkit CSS to modify the formatting of the app for mobile. While you don’t need to implement the complete suite of features in Java (or Kotlin), you do need to set up the webview and enable JavaScript.

In addition, many JavaScript functions do not translate well to the native platform and must be interfaced with Android code to work properly.

After all that, you only get the Android version of your JavaScript app.

Creating a JavaScript iOS App with Webviews

You'll follow a similar process to create a JavaScript iOS app.

You’ll need a Cocoa developer to create a webview app in objective-C and configure a few components to make your JavaScript app play nice with it.

Certain features of JavaScript are known to not work too well in iOS, so be prepared for a potentially long and debugging process.

And even after all this, it won’t exactly be an app.

A web view displays a webpage, without any navigational improvements or visual improvements, making for a poor user experience.

A basic wrapper like this also lacks native app features such as push notifications or analytics.

While you can – and should – implement all these functionalities with dedicated mobile app development, it's likely to be a long and costly process, which defeats the purpose of using webviews in the first place.

A less costly, more efficient way to use webviews and display JavaScript code in a mobile app is to create a hybrid app with MobiLoud.

MobiLoud is based on the same webview "wrapper" principle, except everything is optimized to provide a superior UX - including native mobile features, and all the development work being taken care of (pre and post-launch).

We'll have more on that later.

Read more on the benefits of using webviews vs native apps.

Write Hybrid JavaScript Mobile Apps with JavaScript Frameworks for Mobile

If you want a more integrated mobile user experience than a basic webview, you can create a hybrid mobile app using one of several leading JavaScript frameworks for mobile app development.

Hybrid apps are apps that can run on multiple operating systems, utilizing a mixture of web code and mobile code.

Hybrid development allows you to use the JavaScript code you're familiar with, inside of a mobile app.

There are a number of mobile JavaScript frameworks out there, and each developer has their own preference as to which they're most comfortable with.

Here are a few choices:

Ionic & Cordova: Convert Your JavaScript App to Cross-Platform Mobile Apps

Instead of creating separate webviews for the Android app and iPhone app, you can create a hybrid solution that can work across multiple platforms.

This can be achieved using Cordova plugins along with a front-end framework like Ionic.

Cordova is a solution for linking your web code (JavaScript, CSS, and HTML) to the native API.

This approach saves you from creating separate interfaces for Android and iOS in their respective development environments, as Cordova plugins work across devices.

After all the backend code is rewritten with Cordova plugins, all that is needed is to build a front-end native app to work with this codebase.

‍Ionic is the best option for this, as it is cross-platform and is often used in conjunction with Cordova.

While this method works well and is faster than the previous option, it might be a bit expensive.

You will need to hire developers (or use your in-house team) to create a hybrid app in Ionic and then prepare your web code with Cordova to fit into it.

Let's look at another possibility that allows you to develop apps for both iOS and Android simultaneously.

Build Mobile Apps with JavaScript Using React Native

If the Cordova and Ionic process seems too clunky to you, React Native can be a good alternative for mobile app development.

Based on the React UI library, React Native allows you to build native mobile applications for both Android and iOS platforms – with JavaScript.

The good thing here is that you can build true native apps, with no need to build a container or search around for the right Cordova plugins for every JavaScript functionality.

React Native runs JavaScript directly, so a single codebase can run across both iOS and Android.

The downside here is that you are essentially rebuilding from scratch. Your existing code is obsolete.

Unless you built your app in React (and to some extent, even then), your app needs to be rewritten, and recreated, to take advantage of React Native’s cross-platform functions and classes. Parts of your backend code can survive.

But the UI needs to be rebuilt from scratch.

With this, you can build cross-platform native apps that work and look great on both platforms, but you'll need to go through a lengthy development process.

You're practically creating a new app instead of converting your existing one, and the cost will reflect that.

The good thing is that if your existing developers are JavaScript pros, they will learn how to build hybrid apps with React Native faster.

It will still take months of work though, and cost somewhere in the mid five figures to get to the launch stage on iOS and Android.

Why Your Jewelry Store Should Have an App

At the same time, eCommerce as a whole is booming, and consumers are gravitating towards mobile, with mobile currently making up 38% of all digital spending in the US.

More than two thirds of the US population have made a purchase on mobile at some point, and those who do shop on mobile convert at a higher rate, spend more, abandon carts less and purchase more often.

‍

Having an app lets you provide a better user experience to mobile shoppers, cultivate and retain more loyal customers, and get more revenue from each person who comes through your store.

It also sets your brand apart from the competition by providing a huge signal of trust and authority to potential customers.

Online jewelry brands must establish trust with their customers if they want to survive.

An app is a great way to do that - giving you a direct channel to communicate and build long-term relationships your customers.

Best Examples of Successful Jewelry Apps

For inspiration, check out these examples of branded shopping apps from other successful jewelry brands:

Melorra

A unique feature of Melorra is its 3D viewing option, which allows users to get a 360-degree view of each piece.

Bayam Jewelry

New York City-based Bayam Jewelry sets themselves apart in this crowded market with a convenient, user-friendly mobile app.

They sell a wide variety of diamond and gold jewelry, including chains, necklaces, bracelets, pendants, rings, earrings and watches.

Their app lets you browse their entire collection from your mobile phone, with a smoother browsing experience than the mobile website.

How to Build a Jewelry Shopping App

  3. Convert your website into an app with MobiLoud.

In the rest of this article, we’ll explain each of these choices in more detail, and present all the reasons why MobiLoud is the best way to build a jewelry app in 2025.

Let’s dive into the options now.

Native App Development

Native app development involves creating an application specifically for a particular platform, like iOS or Android, using languages and development tools supported by those platforms.

To build a native app, you’ll need to hire experienced developers to create your app, and build an API to connect the apps to the backend of your website.

All this won’t come cheap, and will come with a lot of overhead once you launch, as you'll need developers on staff to maintain your apps.

Good native apps cost six figures, with six months or more for development time. That's at an absolute minimum.

‍This is just too much to spend and too long to wait for an app, especially when you don't know how much it's actually going to add to your bottom line.

Pros

  • High Performance: Native apps run smoothly as they are built specifically for a single platform.
  • Better Integration: They offer seamless integration with the hardware (like camera, GPS, etc.) and software features of the device.
  • UX: Native apps can provide a superior user experience, adhering to the specific UI standards of each platform.

Cons

  • High Costs: Building separate apps for each platform doubles development and maintenance costs.
  • Lead Time: Each app must be created separately, consuming more resources and time.
  • Complexity: Building native apps is a complex and technical project to manage, even with the main work outsourced.

Overall, unless your jewelry store has millions in funding to play with (and even then), native development likely isn’t worth it.

Your website already does 90% of what your app should. What you need is a smooth UX, the right features, and a fast and efficient build.

This is better achieved through other, less-risky methods.

App Builders

App builders are software platforms that allow users to create mobile apps using drag-and-drop interfaces.

They typically utilize pre-built blocks and themes to allow you to build a jewelry app fast, and without knowing anything about coding.

You compile your app via a visual interface, and when you’re done, hit publish and get working iOS and Android apps.

These have become particularly popular in the eCommerce space, as they are designed for the kind of businesses that don’t have the in-house capability (or the budget) for native development.

However, for how easily these tools allow you to launch an app, you sacrifice a lot of power and flexibility, and you won’t be able to make the same kind of incremental improvements you’ve made to your website over the years.

Pros

  • No Coding: App builders are user-friendly, requiring no coding skills.
  • Fast and Affordable: You can quickly create and publish an app on multiple platforms.

Cons

  • Limited Customization: App builders have a limited set of templates and features, restricting unique customization.
  • Performance Issues: Apps made with builders may not perform as well as native or hybrid apps.

App builders can work well if you aren't too concerned with performance or customization.

But the limitations will likely be too much for jewelry brands, where user experience is crucial, and small improvements have a big impact on revenue.

You'll be leaving a lot of money on the table if you go for the quickest and cheapest option.

MobiLoud

MobiLoud is a fully managed service to convert websites to apps. It lets you keep everything from your website, plus benefits such as increased retention, an app store presence, and push notifications.

Everything works the same in your app as on your website, and the platforms are completely synced.

Whenever you update something on your website, your apps update at the same time.

Your jewelry site already works. It's already optimized, and you’ve spent countless hours and sunk a lot of investment into making sure it’s as good as it can be.

MobiLoud lets you reuse all your hard work, and build on it by providing a native experience for your best customers.

How It Works

  • Finally, after your app is live, we help take care of your app and ensure it stays up to date and bug-free (saving you tens of thousands per year, minimum).

‍- David Cost, VP of eCommerce & Marketing at Rainbow Shops

Launch An App For Your Jewelry Store Today

Building and launching an app for your online jewelry store can be a transformative business decision.

Jewelry brands see the following averages when launching mobile apps:

  • 2.2% of their website visitors download their mobile app
  • 39% higher conversion rate on their mobile app compared to their website
  • 19.5% increase in AOV on their mobile app compared to their website
  • 2.5x increase in monthly visits compared to their website

These results come from the improvements in user experience, plus the retention benefits of getting your brand on your customers' home screens.

You can engage customers and stay top of mind with push notifications, and achieve a huge increase in revenue for a very low cost.

Joomla and Mobile Apps

Joomla is a free, open-source CMS (content management system), powering millions of websites. Joomla is currently one of the top 5 CMS by market share, with a market size similar to Shopify, Squarespace and Wix.

Like any website builder, it lets you create sites that are mobile-friendly, and responsive to work well on different screen sizes.

3 Ways to Turn Your Joomla Website Into a Mobile App

1. Build a Custom Native App

Your first option is to rebuild everything from scratch as a mobile app.

If you really need full control over every corner of your app, then this is the best way to do it. But 99% of the time, rebuilding from scratch simply isn’t worth it.

Why?

Time and money for a start. Building a native mobile app from scratch isn’t cheap. Projects run well into the tens of thousands of dollars. It’s a long journey too, taking months to complete, and requiring constant back-and-forth with developers.

We should say as well that this price and time frame is if all goes well. There’s still the chance that the developer or agency you work with produces something you’re not happy with, and the project drags on.

Don’t forget you’ll also need to build separate apps in separate languages for Android and iOS, which adds to the bill, and the complexity.

At the end of it all, you’ll have two separate platforms to maintain. When you update content on your website, you need to go ahead and update it on the app as well. It’ll be a nightmare to maintain, and a nightmare for your users as well, if they want to access your site on mobile sometimes and desktop others.

“If we had unlimited time and money, we would probably go for a custom native app, but that is half a million to a million a year to maintain.”

- David Cost, Rainbow Shops

2. Use a Joomla Extension

Another option is to find an extension in the Joomla App Directory to turn your site into a mobile app.

Tools like AppsConda are built for turning Joomla sites into apps, while others allow you to turn your site into a Progressive Web App (PWA).

These tools are a lot cheaper than hiring developers to code custom native apps, but they're also a lot more limited.

You're not going to be able to build an app that properly replicates your website's experience. It will be a shell of what your website can does - not a good incentive for customers to download and use your app over the website.

And if you go the PWA route, you don't get the full benefits of a mobile app, such as push notifications and being able to get in the app stores.

If the option is to build an app that is just a shadow of your website, you're better off just focusing on your website alone and forgetting the app.

The best way to turn your Joomla website into an app is with MobiLoud's tech-enabled hybrid app service.

The app has all the same functionality as your website, the same design, and is synchronized with your website, updating automatically whenever your site updates.

To the end user, it will look like a professional, custom, native app. But for you, it's an investment of just a few hundred dollars and less than a month's development time.

That’s it - massively cheaper and faster than paying a developer or agency.

Why Choose MobiLoud?

MobiLoud lets you go live with your mobile apps in weeks, not months, without a huge investment, and without giving you two separate platforms to manage.

The final point is key - it means you add little to no overhead as a result of building apps, with no additional work or cost to maintain your apps.

With MobiLoud, there's none of that. You just manage your website, as you currently do.

MobiLoud is not just a platform or a tool. We work with you the whole way, to ensure the app meets your expectations.

- Nick Barbarise, Director of IT

You can get the same by using MobiLoud for your Joomla mobile app.

The Final Product - Your Joomla Mobile App

Content:
The mobile ecommerce sector is undergoing rapid growth, with a greater number of individuals than ever before choosing to shop on their smartphones. Neglecting the mobile platform and concentrating solely on desktop experiences means missing out on a valuable customer group.

While creating a fully custom native app can be financially burdensome, there's an alternative option: using a JoomShopping mobile app builder. This enables you to develop a fully-functional and premium-quality JoomShopping mobile app with minimal investment.

We'll detail in this post the ways you can convert your JoomShopping store into a mobile app, leveraging its potential to boost your reach, conversions, and overall revenue.

Keep reading to know how.

Why You Need a Mobile App

Mobile's share in the ecommerce market surpasses 72%, having grown from slightly above 52% in a span of five years.

This aligns with the broader pattern of mobile internet usage, which leans more towards mobile as time goes on. Mobile currently holds more than 50% of the market share when compared to desktop and tablet.

Also, 15% of Americans currently rely solely on smartphones for internet access. This indicates that they exclusively use their mobile devices to connect to the online world. This signifies the future of ecommerce, with customers who are not only mobile-first but also mobile-only.

Merely optimizing your website for mobile is a good start, but it falls short. Mobile applications provide a superior user experience, leading to improvements in various critical metrics, including:

     
  • Improved conversion rates
    •  
  • Higher average order value
    •  
  • Increased number of products viewed per session

Additional information reveals that users of ecommerce mobile applications are twice as prone to return to the store within a 30-day period, as compared to individuals accessing it through mobile browsers.

Mobile apps equip you with the powerful tool of mobile push notifications, which achieve average open rates of 20% and click-through rates of 28%.

They're also a remarkable tool to cultivate and sustain a loyal customer base.

Although it is not a strict necessity to convert your JoomShopping store into a mobile app, the advantages it can provide to your business are immense.



Can You Create Mobile Apps for JoomShopping?

JoomShopping is specifically designed for web-based businesses, so publishing a mobile app directly from JoomShopping is not an option. To create a mobile app using JoomShopping, you'll need external aid to fill the void.

Here's a list of options to explore:

  1. Use a specialized platform that has JoomShopping mobile app builder.
  2. Opt for a tech-enabled service that can convert your JoomShopping store into a mobile app.
  3. Work with a team of developers who can create custom Android and iOS apps, ensuring smooth integration with your JoomShopping store.

Can I Turn My JoomShopping Store into an App?

With JoomShopping, you have the option to transform your online store into a mobile app using one of the methods mentioned above.

It is recommended that most store owners steer clear of the third option, which involves employing developers to develop native apps. The primary reason for this is the considerable expense associated with mobile app development. Building a native app alone can range from $30,000 to $150,000. And keep in mind, this cost only applies to one operating system. To launch apps for both Android and iOS (which is highly advisable), you'll need to double this cost.
