OfficeCurb have had success with iOS and Android apps, and you can too. Let’s look at how you can also build even better apps in the next section.

How to build office supplies ecommerce apps

Building office supplies ecommerce apps will allow you to:

  • Improve mobile UX
  • Boost up sales
  • Increase customer LTV

Mobile ecommerce apps are huge for many brands. The ability to send push notifications alone is a game changer.

‍
Sounds good, right? Right. The real issue is building them. It can be a challenge - depending on the route you choose to take.

Speaking broadly, there are 4 options:

  • Native development
  • Hybrid development
  • App builders
  • MobiLoud

Let’s look at them each in turn.

Native App Development

Native app development involves creating an application specifically for a particular platform, like iOS or Android, using languages and development tools supported by those platforms. For iOS, it's Swift or Objective-C with Xcode, and for Android, it's Java or Kotlin with Android Studio.

Pros:

  • High Performance: Native apps run smoothly as they are built specifically for a single platform.
  • Better Integration: They offer seamless integration with the hardware (like camera, GPS, etc.) and software features of the device.
  • UX: Native apps can provide a superior user experience, adhering to the specific UI standards of each platform.

Cons:

  • High Costs: Building separate apps for each platform doubles development and maintenance costs.
  • Lead time: Each app must be created separately, consuming more resources and time.
  • Overall native development is a possibility. Overall though, it's unclear whether its worth it.

Good native apps will cost six figures, and take six months to build, at an absolute minimum.

This alone prices out most small and medium sized businesses, and is a long time to wait before going to market.

There's no need to reinvent the wheel to build office supplies ecommerce apps. What you need is a smooth UX, the right features, and a fast and efficient build. This is better achieved through other, less-risky methods.

Hybrid App Development

Hybrid apps blend elements of both native and web apps. They're capable of running on different platforms using the same code. In the context of office supplies ecommerce apps , they can theoretically be built with frameworks like React Native & Flutter, tools that allow you to build single apps compatible with iOS and Android.

Pros

  • Faster Development: Since the same codebase can be used for multiple platforms, development is quicker than native development.
  • Cost-Effective: The need for separate teams for different platforms is eliminated, reducing costs.

Cons

  • Lower Performance: Hybrid apps can't match the speed of native apps because they need to work across multiple platforms.
  • Expense: though more affordable than native development, building good hybrid office supplies ecommerce apps  will still cost tens of thousands of dollars, upfront
  • Time: though faster than native development, you'll still have a complex project to manage for many months

Hybrid apps are a possibility, but suffer from the same flaw as native development - time, expense, and risk. You will need to be at least somewhat technical to even understand if your idea is feasible.

The technicalities of both hybrid and native development are beyond the scope of this article, we covered them briefly just to make you aware of your broad options.

Next we'll get into a few options for building office supplies ecommerce apps with much lower time and capital investment.

App Builders

App builders are software that allow users to create mobile applications using drag-and-drop interfaces.

In the eCommerce space there are typically two main types.

The first are based on blocks, allowing you to drag-and-drop various pre-built components to define the UI of your apps. Different app builders will have different blocks for specific functions and integrations with some third parties. There will also be some customisation options like colour schemes and logos which will help it to look like something vaguely resembling your main web store.

The second main type of app builder is based on templates or themes. You'll be able to choose from one of their pre-built themes, which come with different styles and functionalities, then add different pre-built widgets and styling to achieve some degree of customization.

In reality, many app builders combine elements of both. What they have in common are pre-built UIs with limited customization options, and a reliance on the APIs of whatever you use as a CMS.

App builders are a popular choice for non-technical individuals or small businesses looking to develop simple apps and there are several in the eCommerce space.

Pros

  • No Coding Required: App builders are user-friendly, requiring no coding skills
  • Fast and Affordable: Users can quickly create and publish an app on multiple platforms

Cons

  • Limited Customization: App builders have a limited set of templates and features, restricting unique customization
  • Performance Issues: Apps made with builders may not perform as well as native or hybrid apps

App builders can be a decent choice, if you aren't too concerned with performance or customisation.

They come with serious limitations though. What makes them fast and cheap - reliance on APIs, templates, and widgets - is also what makes them inflexible and generic.

They force your brand into one of these templates then use APIs to populate them, you'll never have much freedom. APIs only expose certain data, templates can only approximate your brand. If you have a feature on your site and they don't have the perfect pre-built integration available, you can't use it. Even if they do, these various integrations have a performance record that is far from perfect.

APIs also come with platform risk. All it needs is one major update to turn your workflows and app functionality upside down.

“The hybrid solutions on top of Shopify are just too limited. The Shopify API only enables certain things and for all the major providers of hybrid apps for Shopify, they had major limitations that the Shopify API would not let them get around. When our first wrapper app stopped working, we were forced into working with another app provider. That was a year of work, and then we discovered all the different things they couldn’t do because of the Shopify API.”

  - David Cost, VP of Ecommerce and Marketing at Rainbow (read their story)

So app builders are way more affordable, and far simpler to use. You can get an app in just hours, and probably afford their pricing if you have any revenue whatsoever. But, if you have any degree of complexity or performance on your web store, the apps will only be a pale imitation.

We're now going to talk about our platform, MobiLoud, and show you how it gives you the best of both worlds - the efficiency and affordability of app builders with the flexibility and UX of native apps.

MobiLoud is the best way to build office supplies ecommerce apps, let's see how.

MobiLoud

You can spend $150k+ and six months plus building native office supplies ecommerce apps and get something quality, but still limited.

Alternatively, you can tinker with cheap app builders based on APIs and templates, but they’ll limit you even more and could easily be a complete waste of time.

You don’t just want an app, just to tick a box. You want something great. You want something at least as good, if not better, than your main web store. Something that will have a measurable impact on driving up conversions, sales, and ultimately profits.

MobiLoud is a unique answer to the challenge, combining the best of all 3 categories above.

Within just a few weeks you'll be ready to launch on iOS and Android (which we handle too), and going forward we handle all ongoing updates and maintenance.

These brands got top quality apps with MobiLoud, saving themselves tens of thousands of dollars and months of effort. You can too. Let’s build your office supplies ecommerce apps, book a call today and we’ll explain everything.

Article Title: How to Turn Your osCommerce Store Into a Native Mobile App
SEO Meta Description: Launching a mobile app for your osCommerce store could be one of the best things you can do to boost customer retention and LTV. Learn how MobiLoud makes it easy, no matter how deep or complex your osCommerce stack is.
Content:
The Bottom Line: osCommerce has no mobile app solution, and its small ecosystem means nobody else is building one either. MobiLoud takes your existing osCommerce storefront and delivers it as a native iOS and Android app. Every product, every customization, every add-on you've installed over the years works in the app automatically.

Some osCommerce stores have been running for 15 or 20 years. That's not a typo. The platform launched in 2000 and helped pioneer open-source ecommerce before Shopify, WooCommerce, or Magento existed. 

Merchants who built on osCommerce in the early days, and stuck with it, have accumulated something that's hard to replicate: a fully customized store with years of product data, customer accounts, order history, and business logic baked into the code.

If that's you, your store probably doesn't look anything like a fresh osCommerce install. You've installed add-ons from the App Store, written custom PHP, configured payment gateways, and shaped the checkout flow to match exactly how your business works. 

Maybe you've upgraded to V4 on Holbi's Powerful Commerce platform. Maybe you're still running a well-maintained older version. Either way, the store runs and the revenue comes in.

Now you want a mobile app. And you've probably already noticed that the osCommerce ecosystem doesn't have one for you.

Can You Build a Mobile App With osCommerce?

Not through osCommerce itself. The platform's 1,242 add-ons in the App Store cover payment processing, shipping, marketing, and store management, but mobile apps aren't among them.

No Mobile App Builder or Plugin

osCommerce has never included mobile app capabilities. The V4 rewrite brought a modern architecture, PHP 8.3 support, multiple sales channels, and a new template system, but native mobile apps weren't part of that modernization. 

The platform's focus has been on the web storefront, admin tools, and the enterprise features in the Powerful Commerce tier.

A Very Small Developer Ecosystem

This is the practical reality for osCommerce merchants. The active community is small. Around 1,100 live sites and a few hundred active forum members. 

That's not a criticism of the platform. It's just the market reality after 25+ years, with many merchants having migrated to other platforms over time.

What it means for mobile: nobody is building a plug-and-play mobile app solution specifically for osCommerce. The audience is too small to justify a dedicated product. Third-party app builders that serve Shopify, WooCommerce, or even Magento don't support osCommerce.

Custom App Development Is an Option, But a Costly One

osCommerce's architecture can support a custom mobile app. A development team could build API endpoints to expose your catalog, customer, and order data, then build iOS and Android apps that consume those APIs.

For an osCommerce store, expect $60K-$150K for the initial build, depending on how many custom features need to be replicated. Annual maintenance would run $20K-$50K for OS updates, App Store compliance, and keeping the app aligned with your website.

The bigger issue is finding developers. osCommerce's PHP codebase is well-understood by the people who work with it, but mobile app developers who also know osCommerce are rare. You'd likely need two separate teams (web and mobile) that coordinate closely, and the maintenance overhead grows from there.

For a store that's been profitable on a lean budget for years, spending six figures on a mobile app is a hard sell.

Your Options for Launching a Mobile App

Realistically speaking, there are two ways you could launch a mobile app for your osCommerce store:

Custom Native Development

Hire developers to build iOS and Android apps from scratch with custom API endpoints on your osCommerce backend. You’ll have full control over design and features, but high cost, long timeline, and an ongoing maintenance commitment. You'd also be creating a second system that needs to stay in sync with your website indefinitely.

Website-to-App With MobiLoud

Your osCommerce storefront already works on mobile browsers. It handles browsing, search, cart, checkout, account management, and everything else your customers need. 

MobiLoud takes that working storefront and packages it as a native iOS and Android app.

There's no API layer to build. No custom endpoints. No second codebase. The app runs your website, so whatever works on the web works in the app.

Why MobiLoud Makes Sense for osCommerce Stores

The stores still running on osCommerce tend to share a few traits: they're lean, they're practical, and they don't spend money on technology that doesn't pay for itself. MobiLoud fits that mindset.

Every Add-On and Customization Carries Over

Over the years, you've probably installed a stack of add-ons and written custom code to make your store work exactly the way your business needs. 

Payment gateway integrations, shipping calculators, discount rules, product display tweaks, customer account features, maybe some custom admin tools.

A custom app would need each of those features re-implemented through APIs. With MobiLoud, they all work in the app because the app is your website. If a customer can do it on your site, they can do it in the app.

Works With V4 and Older Versions

It doesn't matter whether you're running osCommerce V4 on the Powerful Commerce platform or a well-maintained older version. MobiLoud renders your storefront as-is. There's no version requirement, no compatibility matrix, no plugin to install on your osCommerce backend.

Push Notifications to Reach Your Customers Directly

This is the biggest functional gain from having a native app. Push notifications go straight to your customers' phone screens. New product announcements, sale alerts, back-in-stock notifications, order updates, abandoned cart reminders.

For osCommerce stores that have built a loyal customer base over many years, push notifications offer something email can't match: immediacy and visibility. Your message doesn't compete with a crowded inbox. It shows up on the lock screen.

No PHP or Mobile Development Skills Needed

MobiLoud is a fully managed service. Our team builds your app, submits it to the App Store and Google Play, and handles all ongoing maintenance: OS updates, app store policy changes, and support. 

Your developer (or you, if you manage the store yourself) keeps working on your osCommerce site. Every change shows up in the app the moment it goes live on the web.

Many osCommerce merchants run their stores with minimal technical support. MobiLoud doesn't add to that burden.

Platform-Independent, So Your Future Is Flexible

Here's something worth considering. If you ever decide to migrate from osCommerce to a different platform, your MobiLoud app moves with you. 

The app points to your website. When the website changes platforms, the app automatically reflects the new storefront. No new app build, no re-submission to the stores, no gap in service.

You're not locking your mobile presence to a specific ecommerce platform. You're tying it to your website, which is the one thing that persists regardless of what backend powers it.

How It Works

  1. Try before you commit. Book a consultation and share your osCommerce store URL and we'll create an interactive preview. You'll see your store running as a native app on your own phone before making any decisions.
  2. App build. MobiLoud builds your native iOS and Android apps with push notifications, native navigation, deep linking, and your branding.
  3. Your review. Test the full shopping experience on your devices. Walk through product browsing, cart, checkout. Give us feedback and we'll adjust.
  4. App store launch. MobiLoud handles submission to the Apple App Store and Google Play, including compliance, screenshots, and metadata.
  5. Ongoing management. Your apps are live. MobiLoud takes care of OS compatibility, store policy updates, and support going forward. You focus on your store.

Most osCommerce merchants go from first conversation to live apps in about three to four weeks.

Next Steps

Your osCommerce store has been earning revenue for years, maybe decades. It's customized, it's stable, and it does what you need. A mobile app should build on that investment, not require you to start over.

Custom app development means building a parallel system from scratch, on a platform with a small developer pool, at a cost that likely exceeds what you'd spend on your web store in several years. That math doesn't work for most osCommerce merchants.

MobiLoud gives you native apps built on the store you already have. Same products, same checkout, same everything. Just a new channel to reach your customers.

Book a free strategy call to see your osCommerce store as a native app. No commitment. Just a look at how the process works and if it’s right for you.

Native App Development

Pros:

  • High Performance: Native apps run smoothly as they are built specifically for a single platform.
  • Better Integration: They offer seamless integration with the hardware (like camera, GPS, etc.) and software features of the device.
  • UX: Native apps can provide a superior user experience, adhering to the specific UI standards of each platform.

Cons:

Hybrid App Development

Hybrid apps blend elements of both native and web apps. They're capable of running on different platforms using the same code. Native App Development

Native app development involves creating an application specifically for a particular platform, like iOS or Android, using languages and development tools supported by those platforms. For iOS, it's Swift or Objective-C with Xcode, and for Android, it's Java or Kotlin with Android Studio.

Pros:

  • High Performance: Native apps run smoothly as they are built specifically for a single platform.

Cons:
